title: Negotiating a Raise with the Manager

all.en: I had been preparing for this conversation with my manager for several weeks.
all.zh: 我已經為這場與主管的對話準備了好幾個星期。

all.en: I gathered evidence of my achievements, including the two projects I had led successfully.
all.zh: 我蒐集了自己成就的證據，包括我成功帶領的兩個專案。

all.en: I also researched the average salary for my role in the local job market.
all.zh: 我還研究了當地就業市場上我這個職位的平均薪資。

all.en: When I knocked on her door, she invited me in with a friendly nod.
all.zh: 當我敲門時，她友善地點頭請我進去。

all.en: I stated my request clearly, asking for a fifteen percent salary increase.
all.zh: 我清楚地提出我的要求，請求百分之十五的加薪。

all.en: She raised her eyebrows but did not immediately reject the idea.
all.zh: 她揚起眉毛，但並未立刻拒絕這個提議。

all.en: I listed my contributions, explaining how I had helped the team exceed its goals.
all.zh: 我列出我的貢獻，說明我如何協助團隊超越目標。

all.en: She countered with a smaller offer and mentioned the tight budget this quarter.
all.zh: 她以較低的金額還價，並提及這一季的預算很緊。

all.en: After some back-and-forth, we settled on a ten percent raise plus an extra bonus.
all.zh: 經過幾番來回討論，我們最後達成百分之十的加薪外加一筆額外獎金。

all.en: I thanked her sincerely and promised to keep delivering strong results.
all.zh: 我誠摯地向她道謝，並承諾會繼續交出亮眼的成果。

all.en: Walking back to my desk, I felt my confidence had grown more than my paycheck.
all.zh: 走回座位的路上，我覺得自己的信心增長得比薪水還多。

all.en: That evening, I shared the news with my husband over a celebratory home-cooked dinner.
all.zh: 當晚，我和先生一邊享用慶祝的家常晚餐，一邊分享了這個好消息。

all.en: He poured two small glasses of wine and toasted to my hard-earned negotiation skills.
all.zh: 他倒了兩小杯酒，為我得來不易的談判能力舉杯致敬。

all.en: The next day, I emailed my manager a written summary of everything we had agreed on.
all.zh: 隔天，我寄了一封電子郵件，把我們達成的所有共識整理成書面紀錄寄給主管。

all.en: She replied within an hour, thanking me for being so professional and well prepared.
all.zh: 她在一小時內回信，感謝我表現得如此專業且準備充分。

all.en: At the next team meeting, she publicly recognized my contributions to the latest launch.
all.zh: 在下一次團隊會議上，她公開表揚了我對最新上線專案的貢獻。

all.en: My colleagues congratulated me quietly afterward, and several asked for negotiation tips.
all.zh: 會後同事們低聲向我道賀，幾位還向我請教談判的訣竅。

all.en: I drafted a simple guide on preparation, market research, and clear, confident speaking.
all.zh: 我整理了一份簡單的指南，內容包含準備、市場調查與清楚自信的表達。

all.en: I saved a portion of my first raised paycheck for a long-postponed family vacation.
all.zh: 我把第一次加薪後的部分薪水存下來，準備一趟期盼已久的家族旅行。

all.en: Over the next quarter, I worked even harder to prove that I deserved the new salary.
all.zh: 接下來的一季，我加倍努力，證明自己值得這份新的薪資。

all.en: When the year ended, my manager hinted that another promotion might be on the way.
all.zh: 年底時，主管暗示下一次升遷可能也已經在路上了。

all.en: Three months later, my manager called me into her office for a quiet chat.
all.zh: 三個月後，主管把我叫進辦公室進行一次安靜的談話。

all.en: She told me the leadership team had been watching my recent project closely.
all.zh: 她告訴我，高層團隊一直在密切關注我最近的專案。

all.en: They wanted to promote me to senior associate starting from the next quarter.
all.zh: 他們希望從下一季開始把我升為資深專員。

all.en: I felt my heart leap, but I asked for one day to consider the responsibilities.
all.zh: 我心跳加速，但我請求給我一天的時間思考相關責任。

all.en: That night, I wrote down the pros and cons of accepting the new position.
all.zh: 那天晚上，我寫下了接受新職位的優缺點清單。

all.en: My husband listened patiently and reminded me how far I had already come.
all.zh: 先生耐心傾聽，提醒我已經走了多遠的路。

all.en: The next morning, I accepted the promotion with a clear and steady voice.
all.zh: 隔天早上，我以清晰穩定的聲音接受了這次升遷。

all.en: My manager smiled and said she had never doubted my decision for a moment.
all.zh: 主管微笑著說，她從未有一刻懷疑過我的決定。

all.en: I used part of my bonus to enroll in a leadership development weekend course.
all.zh: 我用部分獎金報名了一個週末的領導力發展課程。

all.en: Six months later, I was mentoring two junior colleagues with the same patience shown to me.
all.zh: 六個月後，我以同樣的耐心指導兩位資淺同事，就像當初有人對我那樣。

all.en: My new role as senior associate brought unfamiliar meetings and unexpected responsibilities.
all.zh: 我擔任資深專員的新角色帶來陌生的會議與意料之外的責任。

all.en: I learned to listen carefully before offering my opinion in any large group setting.
all.zh: 我學會在任何大型會議上先仔細聆聽，再表達自己的意見。

all.en: One Friday, the leadership team invited me to join a quarterly strategy planning session.
all.zh: 某個星期五，高層團隊邀請我參加季度策略規劃會議。

all.en: I prepared three clear proposals supported by recent customer data and market trends.
all.zh: 我準備了三項清楚的提案，並佐以最新的客戶數據與市場趨勢。

all.en: When my turn arrived, I spoke calmly and directly without sounding overly aggressive.
all.zh: 輪到我發言時，我以沉穩直接的語氣表達，並不顯得咄咄逼人。

all.en: Two of my ideas were selected and added to next quarter's company-wide priorities.
all.zh: 我提出的兩個構想被選中，納入下一季全公司的優先事項。

all.en: Walking out of the room, I felt grateful for every difficult negotiation I had survived.
all.zh: 走出會議室時，我為自己挺過的每一次艱難談判由衷感到感激。

all.en: That night, my husband and I shared a quiet dinner with our two junior colleagues.
all.zh: 那天晚上，我和先生與兩位資淺同事共進了一頓安靜的晚餐。

all.en: We toasted to growth, patience, and the courage to speak up at the right moment.
all.zh: 我們舉杯敬成長、耐心，以及在對的時刻勇於發聲的勇氣。

all.en: I went to bed knowing my voice had finally earned a seat at the table.
all.zh: 我帶著一份體悟入睡：我的聲音終於在會議桌上贏得了一席之地。

all.en: A year later, my manager invited me to co-lead an internal mentoring program for women.
all.zh: 一年後，主管邀請我共同帶領一個專為女性同仁設計的內部師徒計畫。

all.en: We focused on negotiation skills, public speaking, and quiet, confident self-advocacy.
all.zh: 我們聚焦於談判技巧、公開演說，以及沉穩而有自信的自我表達。

all.en: Twelve women joined the first cohort, all eager to find their own professional voices.
all.zh: 第一屆共有十二位女性參加，大家都渴望找到屬於自己的職場聲音。

all.en: I shared my full story, from gathering market data to settling on a fair raise.
all.zh: 我完整分享了自己的故事，從蒐集市場資料到談妥一份公平的加薪。

all.en: Several participants later negotiated their own raises with calm, well-researched confidence.
all.zh: 後來有幾位學員以沉穩、充分研究的自信談下了自己的加薪。

all.en: My husband proudly attended the program's closing ceremony with a bouquet of roses.
all.zh: 我先生驕傲地帶著一束玫瑰出席計畫的結業典禮。

all.en: The leadership team praised our work and approved the program for company-wide rollout.
all.zh: 高層團隊讚揚我們的成果，並批准這項計畫推廣至全公司。

all.en: I felt my voice had quietly become a voice for many other women in the office.
all.zh: 我感受到自己的聲音已悄悄成為辦公室裡許多女性的共同聲音。

all.en: That night, I added a new chapter to my simple negotiation guide for future readers.
all.zh: 那天晚上，我為自己那本簡易談判指南新增了一個章節，獻給未來的讀者。

all.en: I went to sleep grateful for every brave conversation that had carried me this far.
all.zh: 我帶著感激入睡，感謝每一場勇敢的對話把我帶到了今天。

all.en: The company-wide rollout reached every regional office within a single ambitious quarter.
all.zh: 全公司範圍的推行在短短一個野心勃勃的季度內就觸及了每個區域辦公室。

all.en: I traveled to five offices to lead live workshops about confident salary negotiation skills.
all.zh: 我前往五個辦公室，親自帶領關於自信薪資談判技巧的現場工作坊。

all.en: Female colleagues filled every workshop, often staying late to share personal stories and questions.
all.zh: 女性同事擠滿了每一場工作坊，常常留到很晚分享個人故事和提問。

all.en: I added new chapters to my negotiation guide based on the most common challenges they bravely shared.
all.zh: 根據她們勇敢分享的最常見挑戰，我為談判指南加上了新的章節。

all.en: An external publishing house requested the rights to release the guide as a small national handbook.
all.zh: 一家外部出版社來信，希望取得將指南出版成全國小型手冊的版權。

all.en: I agreed only if the proceeds funded scholarships for young women pursuing business and leadership studies.
all.zh: 我同意了，但條件是所有收益都要用來資助年輕女性攻讀商業與領導學的獎學金。

all.en: The publisher gladly accepted, and the handbook reached over thirty thousand readers within a single year.
all.zh: 出版社欣然同意，這本手冊在一年內觸及了超過三萬位讀者。

all.en: Five young scholarship recipients wrote me thank-you letters that I keep tucked safely in my office drawer.
all.zh: 有五位年輕的獎學金得主寫了感謝信，我把它們安全地收在辦公桌抽屜裡。

all.en: One letter described how the handbook had given her courage to negotiate her own first job offer.
all.zh: 其中一封信描述這本手冊如何給她勇氣，去為自己的第一份工作開口談判。

all.en: I closed my office door that evening, feeling certain my quiet courage had truly multiplied beyond imagination.
all.zh: 那個傍晚我關上辦公室門，深信自己安靜的勇氣已真正繁衍出超乎想像的影響。

all.en: The following year, the handbook was translated into four languages and distributed across two neighboring countries.
all.zh: 隔年，這本手冊被翻譯成四種語言，並發行到兩個鄰近國家。

all.en: Each foreign edition included a personalized foreword written by a local female leader from that country.
all.zh: 每個外文版都加上由該國當地女性領導者撰寫的專屬序言。

all.en: I was invited to speak at a global women's leadership forum hosted in a beautiful coastal capital.
all.zh: 我受邀在一個美麗沿海首都舉辦的全球女性領導力論壇上發表演講。

all.en: Hundreds of women stood and applauded warmly when I shared my original fifteen percent raise story.
all.zh: 當我分享自己最初百分之十五加薪的故事時，數百位女性起立熱情鼓掌。

all.en: Afterward, three CEOs approached me with proposals to expand the mentoring program into their own companies.
all.zh: 會後，三位執行長帶著提案前來，希望把師徒計畫擴展到她們自己的公司。

all.en: I accepted all three invitations and traveled extensively for the next eighteen months across the region.
all.zh: 我接受了這三項邀請，並在接下來的十八個月裡在區域內各地頻繁出差。

all.en: Despite the busy schedule, I returned home every other weekend to share warm dinners with my husband.
all.zh: 儘管行程繁忙，我每隔一週都回家與先生共享溫暖的晚餐。

all.en: He patiently listened to every story and reminded me that family remained the true foundation of courage.
all.zh: 他耐心聆聽每一個故事，提醒我家庭始終是勇氣真正的根基。

all.en: On my fortieth birthday, my husband framed my original negotiation notes as a beautiful surprise gift.
all.zh: 在我四十歲生日當天，先生把我最初的談判筆記裱框，作為一份美麗的驚喜禮物送給我。

all.en: I hung the frame above my desk, remembering the trembling courage that had quietly started everything.
all.zh: 我把畫框掛在桌子上方，紀念那份顫抖卻安靜地啟動一切的勇氣。

all.en: The following summer, my mentoring program expanded to include young women just entering their first careers.
all.zh: 隔年夏天，我的師徒計畫擴展納入剛踏入第一份職涯的年輕女性。

all.en: We held weekend sessions in a small bright community center near the local university campus.
all.zh: 我們在當地大學校園附近一個明亮的小型社區中心舉辦週末聚會。

all.en: Each participant practiced negotiating fictional offers in front of a kind, supportive peer audience.
all.zh: 每位參加者都在友善而支持的同儕觀眾面前，練習針對虛擬的錄取條件進行談判。

all.en: Many trembled at first, just as I had once trembled inside my own manager's quiet office.
all.zh: 許多人一開始都會發抖，就像我當年在主管安靜的辦公室裡顫抖一樣。

all.en: Slowly, their voices grew steadier as they realized fair negotiation was a learnable craft, not an inborn gift.
all.zh: 她們的聲音漸漸變得穩定，因為她們明白公平談判是一種可學的技藝，而不是天生的禮物。

all.en: I gave each graduate a small framed copy of my original negotiation notes as a parting symbolic gift.
all.zh: 我送給每位結業學員一份裱框的小型副本，內容是我最初的談判筆記，作為臨別的象徵禮物。

all.en: Some graduates later sent photos of their first negotiated job offers proudly hanging beside the frames.
all.zh: 後來有些學員寄來照片，把她們第一次談下的錄取信驕傲地掛在框旁邊。

all.en: One graduate even negotiated tuition for her younger sister using techniques learned during our weekend sessions.
all.zh: 甚至有一位學員運用週末課堂上學到的技巧，為妹妹談下了學費。

all.en: Her younger sister wrote me a short thank-you letter, signed simply with the words your quiet courage helped us both.
all.zh: 她的妹妹寫了一封簡短的感謝信給我，只簡單署名：「您那安靜的勇氣幫助了我們兩人。」

all.en: I tucked the letter carefully inside the wooden box that already held thirty years of brave conversations.
all.zh: 我把那封信小心地放進那個已收藏三十年勇敢對話的木盒裡。

all.en: The next morning, I opened the wooden box again and realized it was finally getting too full to close properly.
all.zh: 隔天早上，我再次打開那個木盒，發現它終於塞到蓋不太起來了。

all.en: My husband built me a slightly larger cedar box that weekend, refusing to throw away even the tiniest scrap of paper inside the old one.
all.zh: 我先生那個週末幫我做了一個稍大一點的雪松木盒，連舊盒裡最小的一張紙條都不肯丟。

all.en: A former mentee surprised me by inviting me to her own daughter's first negotiation workshop, held inside a sunny school library.
all.zh: 一位以前的學員給我驚喜，邀請我參加她女兒的第一場談判工作坊，地點在一間陽光灑落的學校圖書館。

all.en: I sat quietly in the back row, watching three generations of women take notes in the same careful handwriting style.
all.zh: 我安靜地坐在最後一排，看著三個世代的女性用同樣工整的筆跡認真記筆記。

all.en: After the session, the youngest daughter shyly asked if I could sign her copy of the handbook her mother had treasured for years.
all.zh: 聚會結束後，那位最年輕的女兒害羞地問我，能不能在她媽媽多年來珍藏的那本手冊上簽名。

all.en: I signed it slowly, adding a small note beside my name that said your voice matters even when it trembles.
all.zh: 我慢慢地簽下名字，並在旁邊寫了一句小註記：「就算聲音顫抖，你的聲音依然重要。」

all.en: On the drive home, my husband admitted he was secretly nervous about retiring next year and losing his own professional voice.
all.zh: 回家的路上，先生坦承他偷偷有點緊張，因為明年就要退休，擔心失去自己職場上的聲音。

all.en: I gently reminded him that quiet courage worked equally well outside the office, especially in our garden vegetable patch.
all.zh: 我溫柔地提醒他，安靜的勇氣在辦公室外同樣管用，尤其是在我們家後院的菜園裡。

all.en: He laughed and promised to start by negotiating with the neighbor's cat that kept digging up his newly planted spring onions.
all.zh: 他笑了，答應從跟那隻一直挖他剛種好青蔥的鄰居家貓談判開始練習。

all.en: Before falling asleep, I added one final line to my journal: a brave voice planted at home grows the strongest fruit of all.
all.zh: 睡前，我在日記裡加了最後一句話：「在家中種下的勇敢聲音，會結出最強壯的果實。」
